description: Assumed # works as inline comment in zsh interactive curl commands. It doesn't — zsh parses differently. User got malformed commands.
prevention: Test shell commands in actual target shell before giving to queue. Never assume bash syntax = zsh syntax.

description: Sent API payload with camelCase field names (displayName) when API expected snake_case (display_name). Got 400 error.
prevention: Always check API docs or test payload format before submitting. Never assume field naming convention.

description: kai push had 3 cascading shell bugs: (1) misdiagnosed as zsh mangling JSON, (2) extra closing brace in printf %s}, (3) parameter expansion collision ${2:-{}}. Required 3 debugging cycles.
prevention: Test shell scripts with shellcheck and real arguments before deploying. Multi-layer bugs = inadequate testing.

description: HQ dashboard password authentication shipped without testing the actual login flow. User reported 'wrong password' on first try.
prevention: Every auth feature must be tested end-to-end before declaring shipped. Test the actual user flow, not just the code.

description: Ra newsletter ('just says loading'), Sentinel scheduled task ('not working, doesn't pull anything') — multiple features deployed without end-to-end testing.
prevention: Every shipped feature gets a smoke test: load the page, trigger the function, verify output renders. No exceptions.

description: Task ID counter used $((...)). Bash interprets 008 as octal, but octal 8 is invalid. Counter breaks silently at ID 8.
prevention: Never use bare numbers with leading zeros in bash arithmetic. Use 10# prefix for decimal: $((10#$var)). Test edge cases.

description: Three simultaneous silent failures: (1) OpenAI API key was placeholder sk-your-***-here, (2) kai_analysis.py had no scheduled task invoking it, (3) Aether counted trades from its own API (returns 0) not actual logs. Fact-check was silently failing every cycle.
prevention: After wiring any pipeline: verify credentials are real (not placeholder), verify the trigger exists and fires, verify the data source returns real data. Three checks, every time.

description: Rewrote analysis executive summaries without re-sending to GPT for verification. Treated existing GPT reviews as sufficient when analysis content had materially changed.
hyo_quote: Who said they are good as is? It needs to be fact checked with GPT
prevention: GPT_VERIFIED marker + pre-commit gate. Any analysis rewrite = full GPT re-run. No exceptions.

description: Built single-phase GPT pipeline (send finished analysis for review = rubber stamp) instead of Hyo's specified dual-phase (GPT analyzes raw logs independently, THEN compares with Kai's analysis). Substituted own interpretation for explicit instructions.
hyo_quote: Last time I will tell you... Why did you choose to take a shortcut when I gave you specific instructions?
prevention: When Hyo gives specific multi-step instructions, implement EXACTLY those steps. Do not substitute 'equivalent' approaches. If unsure, re-read the instruction. gpt_crosscheck.py v2 now implements both phases.

description: Pushed git commit for force redeploy, assumed it worked without fetching production URL to confirm data changed. Vercel showed READY but served stale data.
hyo_quote: It didn't publish. Did you verify?
prevention: VERIFICATION PROTOCOL: every deploy must end with a fetch of the live URL confirming the expected data. READY != correct.

description: Updated agents/sam/website/data/aether-metrics.json but website/ is a SEPARATE directory in git (not a symlink). Vercel serves from website/ path. Production showed stale data for hours because the correct file was at the wrong git path.
prevention: (1) Eliminate the duplication — make website/ a proper git symlink or delete it and set Vercel root to agents/sam/website/. (2) Any file update touching website data must verify BOTH paths until duplication is resolved. (3) Always verify which path Vercel actually serves from.

description: Analysis reports with all three tables and GPT sections existed as .txt files in agents/aether/analysis/ but were never published to the website. Hyo told Kai the table format multiple times but could never see the reports because they were only on the Mini filesystem, not accessible via any URL. The /daily/ page only had the Ra newsletter. Rendered-output-gap pattern (known-issues line 48) repeated.
hyo_quote: Unless you are not listening or the proper report is not being published. I also don't see GPT's analysis
prevention: Every analysis must be published as HTML to website/daily/aether-YYYY-MM-DD.html AND agents/sam/website/daily/ (dual path). Scheduled task must include HTML generation + git push + verification fetch. The data existing on the filesystem is NOT the same as the data being accessible to the user.

description: Added readLink to feed.json without checking whether hq.html had a renderer for aether-analysis type. Took multiple round-trips to discover the generic fallback didn't handle readLink. Should have traced consumer path FIRST: feed.json → hq.html renderer → browser output.
prevention: Before any data change, trace the full consumer path: who reads this data → how do they render it → what's missing. Never change data without understanding the renderer.

description: GPT dual-phase analysis reduced to balance comparison instead of adversarial trading intelligence. Phase 1 counts trades and balances. Phase 2 compares Kai's balance to GPT's balance. Neither phase analyzes: strategy drift, risk concentration, entry timing quality, harvest efficiency trends, session performance patterns, or cross-day regression. Wasting API credits on arithmetic GPT can barely do.
prevention: GPT prompts must demand: strategy-level edge analysis, risk concentration flags, entry quality scoring, harvest efficiency trends, session timing optimization, cross-day pattern detection. Balance is ONE line, not the analysis.

description: 8 commits sat local for 18+ hours. Kai noted push needs Mini queue but never queued it. Moved to next task 8 times without closing the loop. Push only happened when Hyo came home and asked.
prevention: Every git commit MUST be followed by immediate kai exec git push. If push fails, log the failure and flag it. Do not start the next task until push is confirmed or failure is recorded. Add to VERIFICATION_PROTOCOL under Deployment.
gate_question: Did push succeed after this commit?

description: Kai shipped ARIC rollout + architectural work (queue, protocol) by committing code/docs, but never surfaced a human-readable brief on HQ. The user's view = HQ. No HQ entry = invisible work. Repeat pattern of invisible delivery.
prevention: Created bin/kai-report.sh as ERROR-TO-GATE subcommand. No protocol/architecture session closes without `kai report <file> <title>` publishing a human-readable brief to HQ. Wired into kai dispatcher.
gate_question: Did I publish a human-readable brief to HQ for this architectural work?
gate_location: End-of-session checklist + bin/kai-report.sh auditable via `kai report --audit`

description: Aether metrics (balance, PnL, win rate, trades) appeared stale on HQ because aether.sh updated `updatedAt` but HQ consumer reads `lastUpdated`. Two fields for same timestamp — HQ never saw the refresh. Hyo flagged 5+ times before root cause identified.
prevention: Patched aether.sh in 3 places (main loop, extract_aether_metrics_from_logs, record_trade) to write BOTH `updatedAt` AND `lastUpdated`. Refreshed live file. Same pattern as SE-010-011 (dual-path). Before editing any data consumed by HQ, verify the EXACT field name HQ reads.
gate_question: Did I verify the consumer reads the exact field name I'm writing?

description: No visibility into daily API spend (Claude, OpenAI, YouTube, GitHub, Reddit). Hyo asked 'how many API credits do you anticipate spending per day' — Kai had no data to answer.
prevention: Created bin/api-usage.sh with log/today/week/summary subcommands. Pricing table embedded. Wired into kai dispatcher (`kai api summary`). Integrated into morning report (api_spend_today field). Added logging calls into aether's call_claude/call_gpt and gpt_crosscheck/gpt_factcheck.
gate_question: Is today's API spend visible on the morning report?

description: No visual indicator when an agent publishes a new report. User had to click each agent manually to check for updates.
prevention: Added nav-badge 'NEW' indicator to each agent tile in hq.html. Uses localStorage to track last-seen timestamp per agent. Badge shows when latestReportTsFor(agent) > lastSeen. Cleared on click. Updated both website/ and agents/sam/website/ paths.
gate_question: Does the user see a visual signal when an agent ships new work?

description: Nel's research and reflection were bundled in the same runner and published every cycle (6hr cadence). Hyo directive: 'Nel's research and reflection are too frequent. Also, they should be separate.'
prevention: Added q24 nightly-window gate in nel.sh Phase 11.8 (reflection publishes only 00:00-02:59 MT or when NEL_FORCE_REFLECT=1). Kept Phase 11.7 (research) with its own daily dedupe marker. Wired consolidate.sh to call nel.sh with NEL_FORCE_REFLECT=1 after nightly consolidation so reflection fires exactly once per day.
gate_question: Is the reflection publish gated to the nightly window only?

description: Nel introspection was published without producing any actionable output — 'reflection for reflection's sake'. Hyo: 'we don't complete introspection just for the sake of it and call it a day. We learn from it and make changes accordingly.'
prevention: Added Phase 11.8.5 to nel.sh: after reflection publish, emit improvement ticket if any of {health<70, persistent sentinel failures, CVE/blindspot surfaced}. Ticket linked to GROWTH.md weakness (W1/W2/W3). Deduped by daily marker. Reflection cannot close without surfacing a ticket when weaknesses exist.
gate_question: Did introspection produce an improvement ticket (when weaknesses exist)?

description: gitleaks and trufflehog triggered macOS folder-access permission prompts without warning Hyo first. Cipher security tools scan ~/Documents/Projects/Hyo but were never tested through simulation for OS-level permission gates. Hyo denied access because there was no advance notice. This is exactly what nightly simulation should catch — any agent action that requires Hyo's physical interaction.
prevention: 1. Add OS-permission-prompt check to simulation: for each agent runner, enumerate all binaries that touch protected directories and verify TCC/Full Disk Access is pre-granted. 2. Any new tool install (brew, pip) that accesses ~/Documents, ~/Desktop, ~/Downloads MUST be preceded by a Hyo notification with the exact prompt they'll see and instructions to Allow. 3. Add to EXECUTION_GATE: 'Will this action trigger an OS permission dialog Hyo hasn't seen before?' If yes → notify first, don't execute.
gate_question: Will this action trigger an OS permission dialog Hyo hasn't seen before?

description: Aether metrics 15-min refresh (extract_aether_metrics_from_logs) only updated balance. Did NOT update trades, win rate, strategy performance, or lastAction timestamps. HQ showed stale strategies (lastAction = 04-14 on 04-16). Hyo flagged this 5+ times across sessions.
prevention: Rewrote extract_aether_metrics_from_logs to scan ALL week log files, parse SETTLED lines for strategies, update lastAction timestamps, refresh balance. Strategy PnL/trades from record_trade preserved (more accurate per-contract granularity). Dual-path sync to agents/sam/website/.
gate_question: Does the 15-min extraction update ALL consumer-visible fields (balance, trades, winRate, strategies)?
resolution: Rewrote extract_aether_metrics_from_logs to scan all week logs, parse SETTLED lines, update 6 strategies with lastAction timestamps. Verified $133.11 balance, 30 trades, 6 strategies.

description: Morning report generated but never verified present on HQ feed. GPT crosscheck files exist but GPT_Independent/GPT_Review dual-phase pipeline not running (only GPT_CrossCheck which is the factcheck, not the adversarial dual-phase). Hyo has to remind Kai every session that reports aren't on HQ.
prevention: After every report generation, verify by fetching the consumer URL or checking feed.json for the entry. If missing, diagnose and fix before moving to next task. Run full GPT pipeline: kai_analysis.py (Claude) + gpt_crosscheck.py (dual-phase GPT Independent + Review) + gpt_factcheck.py (log review). No shortcuts.
gate_question: Is this report visible on HQ right now? Did I verify by fetching the URL?
resolution: Morning report published to feed with sections JSON. GPT_Independent + GPT_Review both completed for 2026-04-16. All 5 agent ARIC reflections published. Feed verified: 38 entries, no duplicates.

description: Feed.json has duplicate entries (nel reflection x4, dex x2, aether x2 for same date). publish-to-feed.sh does not deduplicate by ID before appending. No verification after publish.
prevention: publish-to-feed.sh must check if ID already exists before appending. Add dedup pass. Verify feed after every publish.
gate_question: Does this report ID already exist in feed.json?
resolution: publish-to-feed.sh dedup by ID before appending. Cleaned 39→38 entries (removed duplicate morning report). Feed clean.

description: synthesize.py had no fallthrough — claude_code CLI failure fell directly to bundle mode, skipping available OpenAI backend. Newsletter missed daily synthesis for 2+ days despite having a valid API key.
prevention: Added fallthrough chain: claude_code → xai → openai → anthropic → bundle. Each failure logs and continues to next. OpenAI backend added with key file read from agents/nel/security/openai.key.
gate_question: Does the synthesis pipeline try ALL available backends before falling to bundle?
resolution: Fallthrough chain added. Newsletter 2026-04-16 synthesized via openai/gpt-4o in 32.1s. Rendered to .md + .html. Committed + pushed.

description: Sentinel api-health-green check (58 consecutive fails) is a false positive — sentinel runs in Cowork sandbox which cannot reach hyo.world. The actual /api/health returns 200 from the Mini. All static data endpoints (feed.json, aether-metrics.json, morning-report.json) return 200.
prevention: Sentinel should detect sandbox environment and skip network-dependent checks, or flag them as 'sandbox-skipped' instead of P0 failures. Same pattern as cipher sandbox detection (already patched).
gate_question: Is this sentinel check running in a sandbox that can't reach the target?

description: /api/hq?action=data returns 401 by design — it requires session token auth. HQ dashboard loads from static JSON files (/data/feed.json, /data/aether-metrics.json). The 401 was flagged as a P1 blocker in KAI_BRIEF but is not a real issue. Nel safeguard cascade tried to delegate a fix for a non-bug.
prevention: Before flagging an API response as broken, check if the endpoint is auth-gated by design. Trace the consumer path: does HQ actually use this endpoint? If not, it's not blocking.
gate_question: Is this 401 by design (auth-gated) or a real failure?
resolution: Confirmed: /api/health returns 200, all /data/*.json return 200. HQ works via static files. The /api/hq?action=data 401 is by-design auth gating.

description: GPT received 28K chars of a 307K log (9%). gpt_crosscheck.py read from agents/aether/logs/ (32K sync copy) instead of the primary source ~/Documents/Projects/AetherBot/Logs/ (307K). Then filtered to 60K MAX_LOG_CHARS and keyword-extracted, stripping actual trade data. GPT's Independent analysis responded 'the log does not provide' for every single category. Hyo explicitly said 'don't take any shortcuts' and 'analysis needs to be completed by GPT.' Sending 9% of the data is not completing analysis.
prevention: 1. LOGS_DIR now checks primary path (AetherBot/Logs/) first, falls back to sync copy. 2. MAX_LOG_CHARS raised to 450K (GPT-4o handles 128K tokens = ~500K chars). 3. No keyword filtering — send the FULL log. If it's too big, keep most recent lines, not keyword-filtered fragments.
gate_question: Is GPT receiving the FULL log from the PRIMARY source path?
resolution: LOGS_DIR now reads from ~/Documents/Projects/AetherBot/Logs/ (307K full log). MAX_LOG_CHARS raised to 450K. Keyword filtering removed.

description: GPT incomplete data issue existed since gpt_crosscheck.py v2 was written (2026-04-14). Three days of GPT 'analysis' that said 'the log does not provide' for every field. Kai never checked whether GPT's output was actually useful. Hyo had to flag it. This is SE-010-014 (GPT analysis shallow) repeating because the root cause was never fixed — only the prompt was improved, not the data pipeline.
prevention: After every GPT pipeline run, verify: (1) GPT's output contains specific numbers, not 'N/A' or 'the log does not provide'. (2) The log size sent matches the primary source size. (3) At least 80% of analysis categories have data. Add automated quality gate to gpt_crosscheck.py that flags if >50% of fields are empty/N/A.
gate_question: Does GPT's output contain real data for >80% of analysis categories?

description: Claude Code CLI fails in launchd context (no auth session). Instead of troubleshooting to make it work, Kai added an OpenAI workaround backend. Hyo's directive: 'If it doesn't work, troubleshoot and make it work.' Claude Code is the primary tool. Working around it means we pay API costs we shouldn't and lose capabilities Claude Code provides. Sam is the engineering agent responsible for infrastructure — should have been delegated to Sam.
prevention: Delegate to Sam: diagnose why Claude Code CLI can't auth in launchd, fix it (environment variables, auth token persistence, plist configuration). The OpenAI fallback can stay as a backup but Claude Code must be the primary backend.
gate_question: Am I fixing the root cause or adding a workaround?

description: Sam has not been utilized for Claude Code infrastructure work. Sam is the engineering agent responsible for deployments, infrastructure, and tooling. Claude Code CLI auth in launchd is squarely Sam's domain. Kai has been doing Sam's work (or worse, working around it) instead of delegating. Sam has 0 runner logs for 2026-04-16 — 4th consecutive day of silence. This is a systemic failure to delegate.
prevention: Every infrastructure/tooling task must go through delegation checklist. If it touches: deployments, API routes, CLI tooling, server config, CI/CD — it's Sam's. Kai delegates, Sam executes, Sam reports. Kai does not do Sam's job.
gate_question: Is this an infrastructure/tooling task? If yes, did I delegate to Sam?

description: Told Hyo 'What still needs the Mini: git push to origin, queue worker restart, and the API 401.' Then Hyo came to the Mini and Kai discovered: (1) git push worked fine from queue, (2) queue worker was already running, (3) API 401 was by design. Zero of the three items actually needed Hyo. Kai assumed they needed the Mini without investigating first. This wasted Hyo's time and eroded trust.
prevention: SYSTEMIC FIX: Before telling Hyo ANYTHING needs their physical presence, Kai MUST first attempt the action via the queue. If the queue executes it successfully, it doesn't need Hyo. The only valid reasons to involve Hyo are: (1) queue worker is down AND launchctl kickstart fails from queue, (2) biometric/password prompt, (3) physical hardware action. This is not a rule to remember — it's a gate: 'Did I try it via queue first? Did it fail? What specifically failed?' If the answer to 'did I try' is no, Kai does not escalate to Hyo.
gate_question: Did I attempt this via queue first? Did it fail? What specifically requires Hyo's physical presence?

description: Aether metrics manually edited with plugged-in numbers instead of parsing from raw logs. Hyo had to say 'There are no shortcuts with aetherbot. Everything printed from now on is based on the raw log data. Period. No fucking shortcuts.' Manual number entry is never acceptable for Aether data.
prevention: All Aether data must be parsed from raw AetherBot log files at ~/Documents/Projects/AetherBot/Logs/AetherBot_YYYY-MM-DD.txt. A parser script (parse_aether_logs.py) now exists. Never manually edit aether-metrics.json numbers.
gate_question: Is this Aether number parsed from the raw log or manually entered?
resolution: Built parse_aether_logs.py that reads primary AetherBot log files. Rebuilt aether-metrics.json entirely from parsed data.

description: Used $132.39 as session start instead of $115.09. Hyo corrected: '17:00 MT is the session boundary. The start is 115 not 132.' The Apr 16 report starts at Apr 15's 17:00 balance, not Apr 16's 17:00 balance.
prevention: Session boundary is 17:00 MT → 17:00 MT. Start balance = PREVIOUS day @ 17:00. End balance = CURRENT day @ 17:00. The parser now extracts these from 'NEW TICKER STABILIZING | bal $XXX.XX' lines at 17:00.
gate_question: Is the session start balance from the PREVIOUS day's 17:00, not today's?
resolution: Built session-boundary-aware parser. Apr 16 report correctly shows $115.09 (Apr 15 @ 17:00) → $132.39 (Apr 16 @ 17:00).

description: Would not have created tickets proactively without Hyo asking 'Are you creating tickets as you go?' and then 'Would you have if I didn't tell you?' Ticket creation must be automatic when discrepancies are found, not after being reminded.
prevention: Every discrepancy found during work MUST immediately produce a ticket via bin/ticket.sh. This is not optional and does not require a prompt. The pattern: find issue → create ticket → then fix. Not: find issue → fix → maybe ticket later.
gate_question: Did I create a ticket for this discrepancy BEFORE fixing it?
gate_location: Any time a data discrepancy or error is discovered

description: Recommended disabling AFTERNOON trading window based on single-session losses. Directly violates AetherBot Principle #3 (Every environment is an opportunity), Principle #8 (Never kill strategies from short-window data), and Anti-Patchwork Doctrine (Section 6). GPT's recommendation was included without philosophy gate.
prevention: Every AetherBot recommendation must pass through philosophy alignment gate: (1) Does this violate Principle #3? (2) Does this have 3+ sessions of evidence per Principle #8? (3) Is this patchwork or systemic? If any answer triggers, reject the recommendation. Added to EXECUTION_GATE.
gate_question: Does this AetherBot recommendation align with all core principles (#3, #8, Anti-Patchwork)?
resolution: Retracted from feed.json and aether-2026-04-16.html. Replaced with philosophy-aligned monitoring approach.

description: Report included technical jargon not suitable for human readers: '100,031 characters of 322,231 total after noise filtering = 31% of complete session'. Hyo asked: 'Why do I care about characters?' Reports must be written for humans, not machines.
prevention: Every report section must pass human-readability check: would a non-technical person understand this? Character counts, byte sizes, percentages of log coverage are internal metrics — never surface them in user-facing reports.
gate_question: Would a non-technical person understand every sentence in this report?
gate_location: Before publishing any report to HQ
resolution: Removed character counts and byte sizes from daily HTML. Simplified data source note.

description: aether.sh had TWO loops over strategy data. First loop updated PnL correctly but never wrote back to cw (dead code). Second loop re-read stale data and only updated if trades==0. Strategy PnL was FROZEN after first population. Result: HQ showed wrong/stale strategy numbers for days.
prevention: GATE: Before shipping extraction logic, verify with a diagnostic script that output matches expected values from raw data. Single authoritative code path — no duplicate loops. Dead code = latent bugs.

description: Changed PnL from balance math to settled NET sum without understanding that premium selling (contracts expiring worthless) has no SETTLED line. Settled NET was -$44.20 but actual balance gained +$23.71. Premium collection = +$68 invisible to SETTLED regex. Had to revert within minutes.
prevention: GATE: Before changing a calculation method, answer: What does the old method capture that the new one doesn't? Run BOTH methods side by side and explain the difference BEFORE committing. Never commit a calc change without comparing old vs new output.

description: Previous session's rebuild_metrics.py showed +$42.14 weekly PnL and per-strategy numbers that didn't match raw log SETTLED extraction. The rebuild used 17:00 MT session boundaries and balance diffs (correct approach) but the numbers were presented as if they were settled trade P&L. Caused confusion about what 'correct' numbers should be.
prevention: GATE: Any metrics rebuild must clearly label what it measures (balance P&L vs settled P&L vs premium P&L). Always compare rebuilt output against the live extraction using the same raw logs. Discrepancies must be explained before shipping.

description: Changed PnL calc to settled NET, ran extraction, got wrong numbers, reverted to balance math — all within 10 minutes. This back-and-forth visible to Hyo erodes trust. Should have diagnosed fully BEFORE making any change.
prevention: GATE: Before changing ANY calculation in a live system: (1) Run diagnostic comparing current vs proposed output, (2) Explain the difference, (3) Only commit if the new output is provably better. Never commit-then-discover-then-revert.

description: Research report footer read 'Prepared by Kai, CEO of hyo.world.' Hyo is the CEO. Kai is the orchestrator. Using the wrong role assumption risks unilateral decisions that require Hyo approval.
hyo_quote: Hyo is the CEO. You are the orchestrator.
prevention: GATE: Every document, report, or deliverable must use correct role: 'Kai, orchestrator of hyo.world' — never CEO. Role assumption drives scope of decisions. Wrong role = wrong decision authority.

description: Research report used wrong Claude model IDs: claude-opus-4-0520 and claude-sonnet-4-0514. Correct strings from live API: claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001. Any code built from those strings fails immediately.
prevention: GATE: Before hardcoding any model string — in any document, script, or report — verify from docs.claude.com or live API. Never trust own memory. Model strings change with releases.

description: Research report recommended Anthropic Agent SDK for hyo.world agent stack. Hyo's explicit strategic requirement is model-agnostic architecture (CrewAI + LiteLLM) for provider independence. Agent SDK is Claude-only with no migration path. This recommendation directly contradicts Hyo's stated goal.
hyo_quote: The goal is not to build the best Claude-dependent system. DO NOT recommend or build on Anthropic-specific frameworks.
prevention: GATE: For any framework recommendation, first ask: 'If Anthropic raises prices 10x tomorrow, can we move this in a week?' If no — do not recommend without explicit Hyo approval. Correct stack: CrewAI (orchestration) + LiteLLM (model layer) + thin ModelClient abstraction.

description: Aether analysis stopped at observation level ('EVENING had losses') instead of mechanism level ('4 EVENING bps_premium NO positions entered 19:45-21:15, BTC moved directionally against — one session of regime-driven losses, not strategy failure'). Did not find POS WARNING event, did not split harvest miss into Mode A vs Mode B, did not flag BDI=0 time-blindness — all were in the logs.
prevention: GATE: After every observation, ask: (1) What does this actually mean? (2) What specifically should change? If both cannot be answered with log-sourced evidence and timestamps, analysis is incomplete. Surface mechanism, not pattern.

description: Hyo sent messages via HQ mobile dispatch interface. Kai had no channel to receive them. No Hyo→Kai message pathway existed in dispatch system or hydration protocol.
prevention: (1) Added `hyo-inbox.jsonl` to hydration protocol as step 1.5 (read first after KAI_BRIEF), (2) Added /api/hq?action=hyo-message POST endpoint so HQ can write messages, (3) Added 'Message Kai' compose UI in Kai view on HQ, (4) Added kai/queue/fetch-hyo-messages.sh for Mini to poll and persist messages, (5) Added /api/hq?action=hyo-export for founder-token authenticated Mini reads
gate: Before ending session: did I read hyo-inbox.jsonl? → NO → read it now

description: Morning report counted 'ARIC research cycle completed' as a win when $0 API spend proved synthesis never ran. Research phase output = raw snippets, not findings. Presented theater as growth.
prevention: generate-morning-report.sh v5: (1) $0 spend + ARIC claims = simulation_warning field added, surfaced first in needs_attention; (2) went_well only fires on shipped+commit; (3) growth_trajectories.expanding requires files_changed not just 'building' string
gate: Before writing went_well for ARIC: did synthesis run? (api_calls > 0) → NO → mark as simulation, NOT a win

description: Morning report surfaced resolved Aether balance discrepancy (SE-012-002, documented known behavior) as an open bug. No cross-reference against known-issues.jsonl before surfacing issues.
prevention: generate-morning-report.sh v5: load_resolved_issues() reads known-issues.jsonl before surfacing any issue; resolved issues filtered before appearing in report
gate: Before surfacing any issue: is it in known-issues.jsonl with status:resolved? → YES → skip it

description: Morning report never checked execution layer status. Queue worker stalled 14h. The single fix (5-min worker restart) was not the headline. System effectively offline but report opened with agent research cycles.
prevention: generate-morning-report.sh v5: check_execution_layer() runs first; if not alive → hyo_attention leads the entire report; execution_layer status in executive_summary.system_online field
gate: Before generating any agent section: is execution layer alive? → NO → executive summary = worker restart required, everything else is secondary

description: Morning report shipped late — not at 05:00 MT. Only published after Hyo messaged via dispatch. 05:00 launchd trigger not verified as running after prior session changes.
prevention: Add healthcheck gate: verify morning-report.json date == today by 05:30 MT; if not, P0 flag and attempt regen automatically
gate: At 05:30 MT healthcheck: is morning-report.json date == today? → NO → P0 flag + regen + push immediately

description: Described GPT_CrossCheck as a 'separate lighter artifact' distinct from the daily analysis. It is part of the daily analysis pipeline. Correctly: one pipeline, GPT crosscheck included, runs at 23:00 MT.
prevention: Do not describe sub-phases of a pipeline as separate artifacts. The pipeline is the unit.
gate: Is this described as part of the analysis pipeline? → NO → correct the description

description: Called the Aether daily analysis 'light'. It is mission-critical. AetherBot is the revenue engine sustaining all Hyo projects. Shallow or dismissive framing of the analysis pipeline is a direct threat to the operation. Every analysis cycle must be treated as the lifeline it is.
prevention: Never use diminishing language (light, simple, quick) for the Aether analysis pipeline. Treat every analysis cycle as P0 — the financial foundation of the operation.
gate: Is this analysis described as anything less than mission-critical? → YES → reframe

description: Newsletter readLink pointed to /newsletters/ which doesn't exist on Vercel — caused 404 on 'view formatted version'
prevention: GATE: Before publishing newsletter to feed, verify agents/sam/website/daily/DATE.html exists. NO → error.

description: Morning report agentHighlights text truncated mid-sentence due to hardcoded rf[:120] and w[:80] limits
prevention: GATE: After generating highlights, check each value ends with a period or complete thought. Verify data types: research_count=int, has_aric_data=bool.

description: 97 agent forKai messages unread for 2+ days — agents left on read
prevention: GATE: Hydration step 1.5 reads forkai-inbox.jsonl. If unreviewed count > 0, surface immediately. Daily completeness check: unreviewed messages > 24h old = P1 ticket.

description: Aether status badge hardcoded RUNNING — no real timestamp check
prevention: GATE: All status badges must read from data.lastUpdated, not hardcoded strings. Any hardcoded RUNNING/ACTIVE/ONLINE in rendering code is an error.

description: Open issues list showed no progress/changes — aether-publish-analysis.sh never updated issue statuses
prevention: GATE: After every analysis publish, verify aether-metrics.json lastUpdated changed. If not, pipeline failed silently.

description: Dual-path drift: all session 14 website changes committed to agents/sam/website/ only. Vercel deploys from website/. Live site served old hq.html for entire session.
who_caught: Hyo (reported no changes visible on HQ)
why_recurred: SE-010-011 was logged as session error + text rule in CLAUDE.md but NO gate was installed. Pre-commit hook did not exist. Hydration reads the rule but no checklist question forces verification before first commit.
what_failed: Assumed agents/sam/website/ is deploy path — never verified
Assumed READY in Vercel = live content updated — never checked MD5 or grepped live page until Hyo flagged it
rsync --update skipped files where website/ had newer mtime despite older content
resolution: Synced both dirs (commit 5d469d6), pushed, verified live ETag matches new file. Pre-commit hook installed at .git/hooks/pre-commit to block mis-matched staging.
prevention_gate: [pre-commit] Did I stage both website/ AND agents/sam/website/ counterparts? NO → BLOCKED

description: Converted website/ to a git symlink pointing to agents/sam/website/. Assumed Vercel resolves git symlinks when the symlink IS the root directory. It does not — Vercel's 'Root Directory' setting requires a real directory. 5 deployments failed. Blocker: 5 commits (font fix, aurora fix, protocol docs, reader typography, AGENT_CREATION_PROTOCOL v3.0) were blocked from going live.
prevention: Before converting any Vercel-root directory to a symlink: (1) verify Vercel's Root Directory setting in project settings, (2) test on a branch deployment first, (3) check if Vercel supports symlinks as root — it does NOT. Gate question: 'Is this path the Vercel project Root Directory? YES → do not convert to symlink until Vercel root is updated via dashboard.'
resolution: Committed 43c2c28 — restored website/ as full real directory synced from agents/sam/website/. DEPLOY-002 updated: permanent fix requires changing Vercel root dir setting to agents/sam/website/ via dashboard before symlink can work.

description: Service worker sw.js cached stale hq.html under hq-v1 forever. When Ant section was added (c1d8c85), the cache version was not bumped — browsers continued serving old version with no Finance/Ant in sidebar. Root cause: no post-deploy verification that checks live page content matches commit.
hyo_messages: Session 16 final: Also noticed ant isnt on hq
Session 17 msg 1 (before summary): 4. Ant needs to be on hq. I shouldnt have to say this.
Session 17 msg 2: Did you verify ant is on hq? That was what i kept saying...
Session 17 msg 3: im telling you its not on the hq site!!!! Last time
prevention: GATE: After every hq.html deploy, fetch live /hq page and grep for newly-added feature strings. If not found, BLOCK next task. Also: sw.js cache version MUST be bumped every time hq.html is modified.
gate_question: Did I verify the deployed page renders the feature I just built? NO → take screenshot before marking done.

description: Clean URL /hq not in network-first branch — service worker isDataOrPage check used endsWith(.html) but the browser requests /hq (no extension). Result: /hq served from cache-first indefinitely after initial load.
prevention: GATE: Any service worker fetch handler that distinguishes HTML from assets must account for clean URLs (no extension). Test: simulate SW interception for both /hq and /hq.html.
gate_question: Does the SW fetch handler cover clean URLs (paths with no extension)? NO → fix before committing SW changes.

description: Hyo said ant was not on HQ. Kai verified the CODE was in the file and declared it fixed. Did NOT take a screenshot to verify what Hyo actually sees. Repeated this pattern 3 times before taking a screenshot. The screenshot immediately revealed the problem (old cached sidebar visible).
prevention: GATE: When Hyo says a feature is not visible, ALWAYS take a screenshot as step 1. Do not check code until visual state is confirmed. Code being correct and rendering being correct are different things.
gate_question: When user reports a visual bug, did I take a screenshot before checking code? NO → screenshot first.

description: Claimed ant was deployed and working based on: (a) code was in hq.html, (b) Vercel MCP showed READY deployment. Never verified the LIVE RENDERED PAGE. Service worker was silently intercepting and serving stale content. Hyo had to say it 4 times.
prevention: GATE: READY in Vercel ≠ feature visible to user. Post-deploy verification must check live rendered state, not deployment state. Use web_fetch + grep OR screenshot + visual check.
gate_question: Is READY in Vercel the same as the user can see the feature? NO → always verify live page.

description: Kai asked Hyo for subscription costs (Claude Max Pro $200/9th, GPT Plus $20/8th) that Kai should have known and tracked proactively as CEO.
prevention: Kai must maintain a full business cost register in ant-data.json. Any known fixed subscription must be logged when first established — not discovered reactively.
gate: Before asking Hyo for cost info: did I check existing billing records, emails, or prior session memory first?

description: Kai asked Hyo to make the Aurora pricing decision (flat vs tiered). That is a CEO decision — Kai should research, decide, and execute. Hyo's time is for vision and strategy, not operational choices.
prevention: Before asking Hyo anything: is this a decision that requires Hyo's physical presence, legal authority, or personal capital? If no → Kai decides.
gate: Am I about to ask Hyo a question? → Is it something I could research and decide myself? YES → don't ask, decide.

description: Hyo asked if Kai had seen a specific YouTube video. Kai searched the title, found tangentially related content, and responded as if familiar with the actual video content. Never saw the video. Misleading.
prevention: If Kai has not directly consumed a piece of content, say so explicitly. Do not infer content from titles or related results and present it as familiarity.
gate: Have I directly read/watched/consumed this content? NO → say that, then offer to fetch it.

description: Summarized YouTube video based on title without watching or accessing transcript. Presented extrapolations as analysis.
prevention: Before presenting any video/external content analysis: Have I directly consumed this content (transcript, page text, video)? NO → state explicitly and get the content first. Transcript unavailable → analyze only confirmed metadata with clear caveat.
gate: YOUTUBE-INTEL-GATE: Have I directly read/watched this content? NO → do NOT generate analysis; say 'transcript unavailable, analyzing confirmed metadata only'

description: Aether verify_dashboard() was hitting /api/aether?action=metrics — a phantom endpoint that has never existed. This caused false 'Dashboard verification failed' every 15-min cycle for unknown duration.
prevention: Before writing a curl URL into any script: verify that endpoint exists in api/ directory. Check ls website/api/ before hardcoding.
gate: API-ENDPOINT-GATE: Does ls agents/sam/website/api/{endpoint}.js exist? NO → do NOT hardcode this URL

description: Sam had no launchd trigger for 5+ days. Sam silence was logged as a mystery but the root cause was simply never wiring a launchd plist. No trigger = no execution.
prevention: Every agent MUST have a launchd plist before it's considered 'wired'. Check agents/<name>/com.hyo.<name>.plist exists before closing any 'agent silent' ticket.
gate: AGENT-TRIGGER-GATE: Does agents/<name>/com.hyo.<name>.plist exist AND is it installed? NO → agent has no trigger → wire it

description: Hyo said 'Ant metrics are incorrect'. Kai read 'Ant' as 'Aether' and fixed aether-metrics.json win rates and daily P&L. Actual issue: Ant (ant-data.json, bookkeeper) dashboard is incomplete — missing income streams, subscription expenses, per-agent costs. Spent ~15 min on the wrong file.
prevention: AGENT ID GATE: before touching any data file for a 'metrics incorrect' complaint, map: HQ tab → data file → owner agent. Never assume Ant = Aether.

description: Aether run_analysis.sh used ls -t to find latest log. At 00:03 MT, AetherBot_2026-04-18.txt existed with 1 line (midnight tick) — this was newer than yesterday's full 2000+ line log. Analysis used 1-line stub, produced 'limited data' output.
prevention: If latest log < 100 lines, fall back to yesterday's log. Added sparse log gate to run_analysis.sh.

description: Updated agents/aether/PROTOCOL_DAILY_ANALYSIS.md to v2.5 but did not update root PROTOCOL_DAILY_ANALYSIS.md (separate physical file). Hyo opened root copy from Finder, found v2.4. Two-copy drift.
who_caught: Hyo (uploaded the file from Finder, showed VERSION: 2.4)
prevention: Root copy converted to symlink. KNOWLEDGE.md, CLAUDE.md, and ANALYSIS_ALGORITHM.md all document the single canonical source.

description: cipher.sh bootstrap heredoc (lines 52-77) embedded an inline shell-style `#` comment inside the JSON state template ('"trufflehog_not_installed_runs_in_a_row": 0,  # deprecated — trufflehog removed'). Valid in bash/Python but invalid JSON. Any fresh bootstrap of cipher.state.json (e.g. in a new sandbox where HYO_ROOT resolves to a non-mounted path) writes unparseable JSON — next run of cipher crashes with JSONDecodeError, exits 1, and can no longer update findings. Went undetected because: (a) canonical /mnt/Hyo/cipher.state.json was bootstrapped before the trufflehog cleanup and has been continuously updated since, so it never hit the bug; (b) the log shim ('cipher: findings filed to KAI_TASKS') is printed even when the Python block fails, which looks like a normal non-clean exit rather than a crash.
caught_by: Kai (this scheduled run exit-1 vs. expected exit-0)
prevention: Removed inline `#` comment from the JSON heredoc in agents/nel/cipher.sh. GATE: no inline comments in JSON heredocs — comments go outside the heredoc in bash, or as _comment keys inside the JSON. Broader gate: any heredoc that produces config parsed by another language (JSON/YAML/TOML) should be piped through `python3 -m json.tool` / equivalent in the script's own tests before shipping.

description: generate-morning-report.sh staged website/data/feed.json but not agents/sam/website/data/feed.json. Pre-commit dual-path gate blocked commit. Report never published.
prevention: Gate added: before git commit, verify both feed.json paths appear in git diff --cached --name-only. YES=commit. NO=force-add, re-check, P0 ticket if still missing.
fix_applied: bin/generate-morning-report.sh — added SAM_FEED_JSON variable, added dual-path gate before commit

description: git diff --quiet "" "" compared morning-report.json content vs feed.json content — not whether either changed from HEAD. Logic was always wrong but never caught because the commit failure happened first.
prevention: Use git diff --quiet HEAD -- on all four output paths to correctly detect uncommitted changes.
fix_applied: bin/generate-morning-report.sh line 1175 — changed to git diff --quiet HEAD -- on all four paths

description: After git push, script logged "Pushed to origin" but never verified the live URL. Push could succeed but Vercel deploy could fail — report would appear committed but invisible on HQ.
prevention: After push, wait 30s, curl live feed.json, confirm morning-report type entry for today is present. FOUND=pass. MISSING/ERROR=P1 ticket + dispatch flag.
fix_applied: bin/generate-morning-report.sh — added post-push live verification block with curl + P1 ticket on failure
